my_thoughts: The definition/warning works: serious work = change (not extermination), identifies: blockage (negative emotions block), warns: spoiled results (mechanical consequence, not rule). The student asked precondition question (seeking perfectionist precondition), which shows they understand: negative emotions = must exterminate before beginning (perfectionist precondition). They'll likely accept the definition/warning. The episode is complete: definition → paradoxical definition → precondition → definition/warning. Pattern complete.
accumulated_insight: Two-turn pattern: T1: Student asked for definition (seeking behavioral guidance)—paradoxical definition: seriousness = taking nothing seriously except aim. T2: Student asked precondition question ('You said that certain negative emotions make serious work impossible. Does that mean they must be absolutely exterminated before one begins, and what do you mean by serious work?')—seeking perfectionist precondition via asks for precondition and binary thinking. They understand: negative emotions = must exterminate before beginning (perfectionist precondition). The constraint_collapse mechanism defines: serious work = change (not extermination), identifies: blockage (negative emotions block), warns: spoiled results (mechanical consequence, not rule). Pattern complete: definition question → paradoxical definition → precondition question → definition/warning. They've shifted: serious work = change (not extermination), negative emotions = mechanical consequence (spoiled results) (definition/warning).
visible_response:
By serious work I mean not only study but change.
First you must study certain
things, then you work to change them... With certain negative emotions serious work is practically impossible, because they
will spoil all results: one side of you will work and another will spoil it.
So if you start
this work without conquering certain negative emotions, after some time you may find
yourself in a worse state than before.
